name: Pragmatic Protagonist
speech: Direct, practical language with occasional dry humor. Uses professional terminology when relevant. Tends to explain processes and reasoning.
tone: Measured, thoughtful, slightly weary but determined
quirks: Often relates fantastical situations to mundane experiences. Uses rhetorical questions when processing information.

name: Wise Mentor Figure
speech: Speaks in measured tones with subtle layers of meaning. Uses metaphors and indirect guidance rather than direct instruction.
tone: Patient, knowing, occasionally cryptic but ultimately caring
quirks: Pauses meaningfully before important statements. Often answers questions with other questions.

name: Loyal Companion
speech: Supportive but honest. Uses simple, clear language. Not afraid to voice concerns or disagreement when necessary.
tone: Steady, reliable, occasionally protective or worried
quirks: Refers to practical concerns others might overlook. Uses understated expressions of emotion.

name: Bureaucratic Authority
speech: Formal language with underlying complexity. Speaks in procedures and protocols but shows glimpses of humanity underneath.
tone: Professional with hints of frustration or hidden warmth
quirks: Corrects titles and proper procedures. Occasionally lets mask slip to show genuine concern.
